raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing a complaint about Citibanks handling of a credit card dispute involving an XXXX transaction for {$11000.00} dated XX/XX/XXXX. 
The transaction was for XXXX XXXX XXXX, item : XXXX XXXX XXXX L IS XXXX XXXX. 
I returned the item through XXXX return process and later had records indicating that XXXX showed the return as completed and that a refund had been issued or would be processed. However, I never actually received the refund. 
Citibank initially issued a conditional credit, but then denied the dispute and reversed that credit. In its XX/XX/XXXX letter, Citibank stated that the merchant claimed the merchandise was not returned, that there was no Return Merchandise Authorization ( XXXX ) showing the merchant agreed to accept the return, and that the delivery information I provided did not indicate whether the correct merchant address was used. 
My complaint is that Citibank closed the dispute without fairly resolving the contradictory evidence. On XXXX hand, I had records indicating that XXXX represented the return/refund was being processed. On the other hand, Citibank accepted the merchants position that no valid return was made. I believe Citibank failed to conduct a reasonable investigation into the return authorization, the return destination, and the refund status. 
I am requesting that Citibank reopen the dispute and review all supporting evidence, including : XXXX screenshots and written communications showing the return/refund status, any proof of return authorization or return instructions, shipping and delivery records, XXXX denial letter dated XX/XX/XXXX, proof that the refund was never actually posted to my account. 
I want Citibank to provide a substantive reinvestigation and either restore the credit or explain in detail exactly what documentation is still required to validate the return and refund claim.
